topic: Feasibility: Can the mk-spec-memory MCP (37 tools) be replaced by a CLI with zero feature loss?
label: mimo

focus: KQ1 - 37-tool parity matrix with CLI equivalents
noveltyJustification: First iteration — all findings are novel. Independently verified 37-tool count by reading all 5 registry files and classified each tool by daemon dependency level (STATELESS/STATE-EMBED/STATE-WATCHER).
keyQuestions: KQ1: CLI equivalent per each of 37 tools

focus: KQ2 - Daemon-dependency audit across architectures a/b/c
noveltyJustification: Complementary to KQ1 — no overlap. Traced daemon-resident subsystems: job-queue.ts in-process state machine, working-memory.ts session state, embedder warm model server, chokidar file-watcher. Produced per-architecture loss table.
keyQuestions: KQ2: What depends on long-lived daemon and what dies per architecture
answeredQuestions: KQ2: Complete per-architecture loss table produced

focus: KQ3 - MCP-only affordances and CLI replacements
noveltyJustification: Orthogonal to KQ1/KQ2. Identified 6 MCP-only affordances by examining tool-schemas.ts and context-server.ts. Each has a concrete CLI replacement. Bonus: token savings from eliminating tool-schema JSON from every request.
keyQuestions: KQ3: Which affordances exist only because of MCP protocol, and what are CLI replacements
answeredQuestions: KQ3: All 6 MCP affordances mapped to CLI equivalents

noveltyJustification: Orthogonal to KQ1-KQ3. Mapped all 6 integration surfaces (agents, commands, hooks, opencode.json, deep-loop, constitutionals) with ~120 total references across ~29 files. Identified OpenCode runtime change as the sole high-risk/high-effort migration point.
keyQuestions: KQ4: What does migrating current MCP callers entail
answeredQuestions: KQ4: Complete migration map with effort/risk per surface

focus: KQ5 - Architecture comparison, risk register, go/no-go
noveltyJustification: Synthesis of KQ1-KQ4 into architecture comparison. Produced quantitative scoring per architecture, risk register with 7 entries, effort estimates (3-4 weeks for recommended arch b), and unambiguous GO verdict for architecture (b).
answeredQuestions: KQ5: GO for architecture (b) - CLI front-end over existing daemon
